g.text: Why scaling by √d_k prevents attention collapse

text.tspan: ✓ Scaling by √d_k maintains softmax inputs in the linear regime
✓ Enables distributed attention across multiple tokens
✓ Critical for training deep transformer networks
softmax converges to a one-hot distribution.
This causes gradient vanishing during backprop.
Q, K, V: (batch, heads, seq, d_k)
# STEP 2: Scale by sqrt(d_k) for stability
Q, K, V: (batch, heads, seq, d_k)
# STEP 2: Scale by sqrt(d_k) for stability
which handles this internally with fused kernels
